name: Knowledge silos and documentation gaps
description: Multiple problems stem from knowledge being trapped in people's heads rather than documented and accessible. This affects onboarding, departures, and cross-team visibility.
root_cause_hypothesis: No culture or tooling for knowledge capture; documentation is seen as overhead rather than investment.

name: Broken feedback loops
description: Information flows in one direction. Customer feedback doesn't reach engineering, meeting decisions don't reach teams, and support issues don't get triaged to owners.
root_cause_hypothesis: No defined information routing processes; each team assumes someone else is handling handoffs.

name: Missing operational runbooks
description: Critical operational processes (incident response, deployments, support triage) lack documented procedures, leading to ad hoc and inconsistent execution.
root_cause_hypothesis: Processes evolved organically and were never formalized; the team that built them moved on.

name: Planning without data
description: Sprint estimation and technical debt prioritization are done by intuition rather than measurement, leading to unpredictable delivery and growing maintenance burden.
root_cause_hypothesis: No metrics infrastructure for engineering productivity; leadership defaults to velocity as the only measure.
